name: Spicy Chicken Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 pound skinned, boned chicken (cut into bite-size pieces)
3 cups chicken broth with reduced sodium (divided)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and saute until browned for about 5 minutes.
2. Add onion, bell peppers, and garlic; saute for another 3 minutes.
3. Stir in half cup of broth, scraping the skillet to loosen any browned bits.
4. Transfer the chicken mixture into an electric slow cooker.
5. Add the remaining broth, beans, and the following ingredients: dried navy beans through chiles. Cover with the lid and cook on high heat setting for 6 hours or until beans are tender.
6. Serve chili in bowls and top each serving with shredded cheese.
Yield: 6 servings (serving size: 1 1/2 cups chili and 1 tablespoon cheese).
Per serving: 2 P/M, 1 FR/V, 1 B.
CAL 323 (23% from fat); PRO 28.4 g; FAT 8.3 g (sat 2.8 g); CARB 35.1 g; FIB 5.8 g; CHOL 68 mg; IRON 5.3 mg; SOD 534 mg; CALC 178 mg.
